ops.body: T3.1 validation (step 7) complete: the REJECTED candidate's converted artifact scored over the FULL 628-row frozen watcher frame through the actual served stack (4-bit MLX + adapter, f32 normalization, row-at-a-time with per-row cache clear): served_auc=0.9291 (335 positives), fresh_epoch_floor_ok=true, prob range 3.6e-5–0.964 mean 0.291, 1551s at 0.405 rows/s, RSS flat 2.9GB (no Jetsam — the E12 kill was unbounded growth; bounded scoring holds flat with the watch daemon serving concurrently). Conclusion: the old parity gate rejected a genuinely good candidate — under eval-what-you-serve it clears the floor decisively (fresh-epoch = floor-only; no incumbent probs on the clean frame, so no coverage sign-test). T3.1 commits on the cc-steer tree: 41bf3215 already reviewed (C0 round); 41950b4f (f32 nosteer softmax) + a2804814 (eval-what-you-serve gate: convert-before-gate, _score_frame_local, parity gate deleted, serving-drift diagnostic persisted on every outcome, parity_rows/parity_tolerance → diagnostic_rows/diagnostic_tolerance in recipe+packaged asset) — full pytest green, scoped Linux ty clean. Next: sol finder/refuter review of the two T3.1 commits (row/label alignment in the local scorer is the top correctness risk; also probs-digest bookkeeping vs the fresh-epoch crash guard, budget_held preservation, threshold-from-served-probs, no dangling parity refs, wheel packaging of the renamed recipe fields) → fix round if needed → the REAL `retrain --component watcher --fresh-epoch` run (new Tinker spend under the $15 cap, my go/no-go) → promote → daemon serves → push + release cc-steer v0.14 so the Sunday 04:00 PyPI lane runs the new gate (without the release it would re-run the deleted parity gate from the old wheel).
